RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER ENFORCING PLAINTIFFS STIPULATION AND PRECLUDING PLAINTIFF AND HIS EXPERTS FROM ALLEGING THAT MULTIPLE MYELOMA IS RELATED TO ASBESTOS EXPOSURE; AND JOINDERS TO THE MOTION

Hearing held. (At the trial call parties stipulated to extend the 120th pref. trial date for 15 days to June 12, 2015.) The Court made the following ruling re: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for an order enforcing plaintiff's stipulation and precluding plaintiff and his experts from alleging that multiple myeloma is related to asbestos exposure. Defendants are allowed to depose Dr. Kagan for 5 hours re: myeloma only and on shortened notice which is stipulated by plaintiff. Defendants may ask for extension if needed. Defendants have until 2 days before trial to designate their expert(s). Discovery cut-off is ordered extended to June 12. The Court further set a Case Management Conference / Trial Call on May 29, 2015 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 503. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsay Weiss of COOLEY MANION JONES KUROWSKI LLP (213-622-7300), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Delmar Stephens

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the exposure issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Davis v. Foster Wheeler Energy Corp., (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 731, does not bar plaintiff's negligence and/or premises owner/contractor liability claims as a matter of law. Although defendant's brief argues plaintiff's strict liability cause of action should be summarily adjudicated, defendant failed to properly make adjudication of strict liability an issue. Neither defendant's notice of motion nor its separate statement laid it out as an adjudication issue. (See CRC 3.1350(b), (d).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol L. Schilp of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 648 (503/TLJ)

Defendant Kaiser Ventures Llc¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeremy D. Huie of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4429), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Ventures LLC
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Delmar Stephens

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER VENTURES LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Ventures LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (Workers' Compensation) and denied as to issues 2 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and 3 (strict liability). As to issue 1, plaintiff represents that he does not contend that defendant is liable for injuries arising out of his employment by defendant thus this issue is unopposed. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action. Defendant's failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff's factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support these claims precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of these causes of action. As to issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his strict liability cause of action. Defendant's failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff's factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support this cause of action precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of this cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol L. Schilp of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 648 (503/TLJ)
